meta.title: PROs and Research Supporting Dose Recommendations for Symptoms
meta.description: Cannabinoid dose recommendations supported by clinical research and patient-reported outcomes (PROs)
meta.source: Justin Kander, Whole Health Oncology research compilation
meta.notes: This corpus seeds the Research Agent and the Dosing Recommendation Engine. Studies are indexed by symptom category with structured dosing data (mg, frequency, duration, delivery method, cannabinoid ratios) and traceable citations (PMIDs).

research.title: Cannabidiol for Scan-Related Anxiety in Women With Advanced Breast Cancer
research.summary: A single 400mg dose of isolated CBD reduced anxiety before scans

research.title: Phase IIb Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Dose-Escalating, Double-Blind Study of Cannabidiol Oil (MedCan1-CBD)
research.summary: Median 400mg/day CBD for 28 days was not effective for anxiety, depression, or quality of life

research.title: Acute and Extended Anxiolytic Effects of Cannabidiol in Cannabis Flower
research.summary: All flower types reduced anxiety, but 24% CBD flower was most effective for lower anxiety and reduced tension

research.title: Acute effects of cannabigerol on anxiety, stress, and mood
research.summary: A single 20mg CBG dose reduced anxiety and stress within 20 minutes

research.title: Medical Cannabis in Patients with Chronic Pain: Effect on Pain Relief, Pain Disability, and Psychological aspects
research.summary: Average 10mg/day THC for 12 months substantially improved anxiety and depression

research.title: The effects of cannabidiol on worry and anxiety among high trait worriers
research.summary: No acute effect at 300mg or 50mg CBD, but 300mg/day over 2 weeks did reduce anxiety symptoms

research.title: Cannabidiol for Treatment-Resistant Anxiety Disorders in Young People
research.summary: Up to 800mg/day CBD for 12 weeks reduced anxiety by 42.6% and significantly reduced depression

research.title: Cannabidiol as a personalized treatment for anxiety: clinical cases in Mexico
research.summary: 20-33.3mg/day CBD helped anxiety and sleep; one patient needed 80mg/day

research.title: The Effectiveness and Adverse Events of CBD and THC in Treatment of Anxiety Disorders in a PTSD Subpopulation
research.summary: Median 50mg/day CBD and 4.4mg/day THC improved anxiety, depression, fatigue, and social activities

research.title: Oral Cannabis Extract for Secondary Prevention of Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting
research.summary: 2.5mg THC + 2.5mg CBD 3x/day for 6 days significantly reduced CINV

research.title: Oral THC:CBD cannabis extract for refractory chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting
research.summary: 1-4 capsules of 2.5mg THC + 2.5mg CBD 3x/day reduced nausea and vomiting

research.title: Oral cannabidiol for prevention of acute and transient chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy
research.summary: 150mg CBD 2x/day effective for lowering chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy

research.title: Multicenter study of THC:CBD extract and THC extract in intractable cancer-related pain
research.summary: THC:CBD effective for pain — 43% showed >30% pain reduction vs 21% placebo. THC alone showed nonsignificant change.

research.title: A randomized trial of medical cannabis in patients with stage IV cancers
research.summary: Average 34mg/day THC and 17mg/day CBD improved pain control and reduced opioid use

research.title: Smoked cannabis for chronic neuropathic pain
research.summary: Single inhalation of 25mg of 9.4% THC cannabis 3x/day (7mg total) for 5 days reduced pain

research.title: IPElife: Phase III trial of cannabis sativa extract in metastatic lung cancer
research.summary: 100mg/day CBD reduced pain scores in metastatic lung cancer

research.title: Treating insomnia symptoms with medicinal cannabis: a randomized crossover trial (ZTL-101)
research.delivery: sublingual whole-plant extract (20mg/mL THC, 2mg/mL CBN, 1mg/mL CBD)
research.summary: Half benefited at low dose, half needed high dose. Faster sleep onset, longer sleep, improved quality.

research.title: Medicinal cannabis improves sleep in adults with insomnia
research.summary: 55% improved at max dose (15mg THC/22.5mg CBD), 20% at 4-6mg THC, 25% at 8-12mg THC

research.title: Eight Weeks of Daily Cannabidiol Supplementation Improves Sleep Quality and Immune Cell Cytotoxicity
research.summary: 50mg/day CBD over 8 weeks improved sleep quality and enhanced NK immune cell function

research.title: A phase II study of delta-9-THC for appetite stimulation in cancer-associated anorexia
research.summary: 2.5mg THC 3x/day for 4 weeks effective for appetite stimulation

research.title: Prevention of Taste Alterations in Patients with Cancer Receiving Chemotherapy
research.summary: 150mg CBD 2x/day may improve taste perception during chemotherapy

research.title: The Effects of Consuming Cannabis Flower for Treatment of Fatigue
research.summary: 91.94% of people experienced decreased fatigue

research.title: Ingestion of a THC-Rich Cannabis Oil in People with Fibromyalgia
research.summary: Average ~4.4mg/day THC reduced fatigue after 8 weeks

research.title: Vaporized Cannabis versus Placebo for Acute Migraine
research.summary: 6% THC/11% CBD was most effective for migraine pain relief, freedom, and symptom freedom at 2 hours. THC/CBD combination outperformed THC-only and CBD-only.

research.title: Relationship Between Recreational Marijuana Use and Bowel Function
research.summary: Recent marijuana use associated with 30% decreased odds of constipation
